mnemonics: Every Planar Graph Can Sit Properly (Euler's Formula, Planarity, Graph Representation, Combinatorial Map, Straight Line, Planar Embedding)
keyPoints: A planar graph can be represented using combinatorial maps which encode the cyclic order of edges around vertices.
Planar graphs have a straight-line drawing representation where every edge is a straight line segment (Fáry's Theorem).
Euler's Formula (V - E + F = 2) relates the number of vertices, edges, and faces in a connected planar graph.
commonMistakes: Assuming all graph representations (like adjacency matrices) clearly reveal planarity; special embeddings are often needed.
Confusing planar graphs with their embeddings; a graph is planar if such an embedding exists, but it may have multiple representations.
